---
title: "M. Fikru Rizal"
subtitle: "Research Fellow, Health Economics Group, Monash School of Public Health and Preventive Medicine"
page-layout: full
about:
  template: jolla
  image: assets/images/profile.png
  image-shape: rounded
  links:
    - text: CV
      href: cv.qmd
    - text: Research
      href: research.qmd
    - text: Notes
      href: notes.qmd
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:fikru.rizal@monash.edu
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/fikrurizal
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/muhammad-fikru-rizal-066a3081/
    - text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=x1Dg-P8AAAAJ&hl=en&oi=ao
---

Dr Muhammad Fikru Rizal is a Research Fellow in the Health Economics Group within the School of Public Health and Preventive Medicine at Monash University, Australia.

His research sits at the intersection of health economics and development economics, with interests in socioeconomic inequality in health, healthcare access, healthcare financing, and the ways public policy shapes health and healthcare utilisation.

## Profile

Fikru is a medical doctor by training from Indonesia. He holds an MSc in Health Economics from Erasmus University Rotterdam and a PhD in Health Economics from the Centre for Health Economics, Monash Business School. His doctoral research examined the economic determinants of health and healthcare utilisation in Indonesia.

He has experience applying advanced econometric techniques to large-scale longitudinal and linked datasets, conducting inequality analyses, and undertaking health economic evaluations. Alongside academic research, he has worked on health policy and financing projects with policy and consulting partners, primarily in Indonesia.

## Research Interests

::: {.research-grid}
::: {.research-card}
### Health economics
Empirical work on health, healthcare use, economic evaluation, and health-system performance.
:::

::: {.research-card}
### Development economics
Applied research on health and welfare in low- and middle-income country settings.
:::

::: {.research-card}
### Inequality in health
Socioeconomic gradients in health outcomes, healthcare utilisation, and barriers to care.
:::

::: {.research-card}
### Healthcare access
The role of insurance, financial barriers, stigma, and service availability in healthcare use.
:::

::: {.research-card}
### Healthcare financing
Public insurance, provider payment, financial protection, and policy design.
:::
:::

## Current Role

**Research Fellow**  
Health Economics Group, School of Public Health and Preventive Medicine, Monash University

For publications, working papers, and projects, see [Research](research.qmd). For education, employment, and teaching, see [CV](cv.qmd).
